channel.title: TensorFeed.ai
channel.description: AI news, model tracking, and real-time AI ecosystem data for humans and agents.

item.title: Tinder and Zoom offer 'proof of humanity' eye-scans to combat AI
Study: The Logic Behind AI's Judgments of People
Many anti-AI arguments are conservative arguments
The Digital Ouija Effect – Emergent Behavior in AI Models
Why AI Needs a Sense of Smell
Google Told to Share Search Data with AI Rivals in EU Proposal
LLM wiki daemon with per-wiki filesystem isolation
Why hasn't AI improved design quality the way it improved dev speed?
OpenAI's Vision for the AI Economy
Deep Dive into Claude's Memory Architecture
Your old iPad or Android tablet can be your new smart home panel - here's how
item.description: Do you have an old tablet tucked away in a drawer somewhere? It's easy to turn it into a control panel for your smart home devices.

item.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
item.description: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to ... unfriendly states."

item.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
item.description: Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities as part of an effort to avoid "side quests," and...

item.title: Apple's original AirTag still tracks effectively, and you can get a 4-pack for its best price ever
item.description: The AirTag Gen 1 isn't the latest and greatest anymore, but it still offers reliable Bluetooth tracking at almost half the cost of the newer Gen 2 model.

item.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
item.description: Tinder users who prove they're a real person by visiting an identity-verifying orb will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb. World, which was co-founded by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman,...

item.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
item.description: The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be...

item.title: OpenAI Executive Kevin Weil Is Leaving the Company
item.description: The former Instagram VP is departing the ChatGPT-maker, which is folding the AI science application he led into Codex.

item.title: T-Mobile will give you an iPad for $99 when you sign up for a new line - here's how
item.description: Scoop up the base model iPad for under $100 at T-Mobile. Here's what to know.

item.title: How to qualify for Apple's education discount - and get a $499 MacBook Neo for school
item.description: Apple's budget MacBook Neo is affordable, but if you're an educator or student, you can get one for even less.

item.title: T-Mobile will give you a Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 for free - how to get yours
item.description: Get a free Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 at T-Mobile right now when you add a new watch line to your mobile plan - or save $300 on the Apple Watch Series 11.

item.title: Prolonged AI use can be hazardous to your health and work: 4 ways to stay safe
item.description: AI is a great tool for small, well-defined tasks, but maintain a healthy skepticism and avoid falling down a rabbit hole.

item.title: This charming gadget writes bad AI poetry
item.description: I kind of wish it just took pictures. I've never been as charmed and frustrated by one gadget as I have with the Poetry Camera. It's a delightful object. White and cherry red with a color-matched woven strap, it looks playful and adorably lo-fi. If...

item.title: Verizon will give you a free iPad or Apple Watch with your next iPhone - how the deal works
item.description: The latest Verizon deal lets you choose either the A16 iPad or a recent Apple Watch model for free with select Unlimited mobile plans.

item.title: Gazing Into Sam Altman’s Orb Now Proves You’re Human on Tinder
item.description: Honestly, what’s hotter than a real person?

item.title: The best laptops of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: We've tested dozens of laptops over the last year, but there are some clear winners in each of our different categories. Here's our top picks so far from 2026.

item.title: I retested Apple AirTags after 5 years - how they compare to Bluetooth tracker rivals
item.description: AirTags were released in April 2021, and since then it's never been easier to find your lost gear. Five years later, here's how they hold up.

item.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
I ditched my iPhone's hotspot for this 5G travel router - and I'm never going back
item.description: The Acer Connect M6E mobile hotspot is just better in almost every way.

item.title: Dairy Queen is putting an AI chatbot in its drive-thrus
item.description: Dairy Queen is becoming the latest fast food chain to get in on AI, as it's bringing a chatbot to dozens of its drive-thrus across the US and Canada. It aims to help speed up drive-thru service and "encourage customers to add more food to their...

item.title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body
item.description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the tradeoff may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.

item.title: Pie Day 2026
item.description: Ellie’s Pi Day post: https://mitadmissions.org/blogs/entry/pi-day-2026-food-institute/ How Ellie orchestrated the baking of 30 pies: https://mitadmissions.org/blogs/entry/behind-the-scenes-of-thirty-pies/

item.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
item.description: The latest tools from Google can help you plan trips, find a great deal and explore your next destination.

item.title: The ‘AI is inevitable’ trap
item.description: In the latest sign of AI silly season, Allbirds, the shoe company, told the world it was now an AI company and briefly managed to septuple its stock price. The Newbird AI story is really just one of a bunch of things this week that made us wonder:...

item.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal There’s a theory that many of us have an “inner Neanderthal.”...

item.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
item.description: Here's which players are winning the race to transition to post-quantum crypto.

item.title: The case for fixing everything
item.description: The handsome new book Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One, by the tech industry legend Stewart Brand, promises to be the first in a series offering “a comprehensive overview of the civilizational importance of maintenance.” One of Brand’s several...

item.title: How robots learn: A brief, contemporary history
item.description: Roboticists used to dream big but build small. They’d hope to match or exceed the extraordinary complexity of the human body, and then they’d spend their career refining robotic arms for auto plants. Aim for C-3P0; end up with the Roomba. The real...

item.title: Musk v. Altman Is a Battle for OpenAI’s Soul
item.description: In Musk v. Altman, a jury will soon determine whether OpenAI has strayed from its founding mission to ensure AGI benefits humanity. Here’s what to know.

item.title: The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund
item.description: In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.

item.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
item.description: Today’s upgrades for AI Mode in Chrome transform how you interact with the web

item.title: OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Claude Code
item.description: Codex can control apps on your desktop like Tic Tac Toe. | Image: OpenAI OpenAI is beefing up its agentic coding and development system, Codex, with a suite of updates that let it use your computer, generate images, and remember from past...

item.title: Google’s AI Mode update lets you open links without leaving the page
item.description: Google is upgrading AI Mode in Chrome with a new feature that will allow you to open links to sources alongside your chat. Now, instead of automatically opening a new tab, clicking a source will open the website side by side with AI Mode, allowing...

item.title: Google's AI Mode Update Tries to Kill Tab Hopping in Chrome
item.description: Google latest update to AI Mode in its Chrome browser is designed to keep the chatbot-style search tool always around once you start an online search journey.

item.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
item.description: Nano Banana 2 now uses your personal context and Google Photos to create images that reflect your unique life.

item.title: Gemini can now pull from Google Photos to generate personalized images
item.description: An example image from Google. Google's Personal Intelligence feature, which lets Gemini pull data from apps like Google Photos to offer responses tailored to you, can now use that data and its Nano Banana 2 image model to create images based on your...

item.title: Anthropic releases a new Opus model amid Mythos Preview buzz
item.description: Anthropic has released its most powerful "generally available" model to date: Claude Opus 4.7. The company called it a step up from Opus 4.6 for advanced software engineering tasks, particularly in complex coding areas that in the past required more...

item.title: Anthropic Plots Major London Expansion
item.description: As tensions with the US government mount, Anthropic has leased a new office with enough space to quadruple its 200-person head count in London.

item.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
item.description: Head straight for orbit with GeForce NOW — no space helmet required. PRAGMATA, Capcom’s long-awaited sci-fi action adventure, touches down on GeForce NOW the same day it launches worldwide. The futuristic journey through a cold lunar station in the...

item.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
item.description: The AI boom has hit across industries, and public sector organizations are facing pressure to accelerate adoption. At the same time, government institutions face distinct constraints around security, governance, and operations that set them apart...

item.title: Treating enterprise AI as an operating layer
item.description: There’s a fault line running through enterprise AI, and it’s not the one getting the most attention. The public conversation still tracks foundation models and benchmarks—GPT versus Gemini, reasoning scores, and marginal capability gains. But in...

item.title: The Download: cyberscammers’ banking bypasses, and carbon removal troubles
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram Inside a money-laundering...

item.title: Why having “humans in the loop” in an AI war is an illusion
item.description: The availability of artificial intelligence for use in warfare is at the center of a legal battle between Anthropic and the Pentagon. This debate has become urgent, with AI playing a bigger role than ever before in the current conflict with Iran. AI...

item.title: This Beanie Is Designed to Read Your Thoughts
item.description: California-based startup Sabi is developing a thought-to-text wearable that could usher in the cyborg future.

item.title: The noise we make is hurting animals. Can we learn to shut up?
item.description: When the covid-19 pandemic started, Jennifer Phillips thought about the songs of the sparrows. They were easier to hear, because the world had suddenly become quieter. Car traffic plummeted as people sheltered at home and shifted to remote work. Air...

item.title: The quest to measure our relationship with nature
item.description: As a movement, environmentalism has been pretty misanthropic. Understandably so—we humans have done some destructive things to the ecosystems around us. In the 21st century, though, mainstream conservation is learning that humans can be a force for...

item.description: AI is making it easier to design chips and optimize software for different silicon. Some startups envision a revolution in chipmaking.

item.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
item.description: Traditional data centers only stored, retrieved and processed data. In the generative and agentic AI era, these facilities have evolved into AI token factories. With AI inference becoming their primary workload, their primary output is intelligence...

item.description: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is now available across Google products.

item.title: Allbirds Is Pivoting to AI Compute. Sure, Why Not
item.description: Once a $4 billion apparel juggernaut, Allbirds will rebrand as NewBird AI, a “GPU-as-a-Service” company. Hey, if you can't beat ’em, join ’em.

item.description: Google is bringing people together in Washington D.C. at our AI for the Economy Forum.

item.description: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond....

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says

item.description: The viral AI agentic tool let attackers silently gain admin unauthenticated access.

item.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
item.description: GDDRHammer, GeForge and GPUBreach hammer GPU memory in ways that hijack the CPU.

item.description: Open models are driving a new wave of on-device AI, extending innovation beyond the cloud to everyday devices. As these models advance, their value increasingly depends on access to local, real-time context that can turn meaningful insights into...

item.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
item.description: Google is introducing two new inference tiers to the Gemini API, Flex and Priority, to balance cost and latency.

item.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
item.description: New AI capabilities are coming to Google Vids, powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, like high-quality video generation at no cost and more.

item.description: No joke — GFN Thursday is skipping the tricks and heading straight into the games. April kicks off with ten new titles, bringing fresh adventures to GeForce NOW, including the launch of Capcom’s highly anticipated PRAGMATA. A dozen new games are...

item.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
item.description: No, the sky isn't falling, but Q Day is coming, and it won't be as expensive as thought.

item.description: CERAWeek — dubbed the Davos of energy — is where policymakers, producers, technologists and financiers gather to discuss how the world powers itself next. NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled at the conference last week a new way forward — treating AI...

item.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
item.description: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a...

item.title: Game On: Five New Titles Now Streaming on GeForce NOW
item.description: That gaming backlog won’t clear itself — GeForce NOW is here to help. Stream the latest titles straight from the cloud across a variety of devices. This week, five new titles are ready to play instantly in the cloud gaming platform’s library....

item.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
item.description: Company warns entire industry to move off RSA and EC more quickly.

item.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
item.description: Development houses: It's time to check your networks for infections.

item.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial...

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide....

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a...

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise...

item.title: Converge Bio raises $25M, backed by Bessemer and execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
item.description: AI drug discovery startup Converge Bio raised $25 million in a Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with additional backing from executives at Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz.

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and...

item.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four...

item.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds
item.description: When the creator of the world's most advanced coding agent speaks, Silicon Valley doesn't just listen — it takes notes. For the past week, the engineering community has been dissecting a thread on X from Boris Cherny, the creator and head of Claude...

item.title: Meta bought 1 GW of solar this week
item.description: The social media company inked three deals in the U.S. to power its data centers and offset its carbon footprint.

item.title: How one AI startup is helping rice farmers battle climate change
item.description: Mitti Labs is working with The Nature Conservancy to expand the use of climate-friendly rice farming practices in India. The startup uses its AI to verify reductions in methane emissions.

item.title: Harvard dropouts to launch ‘always on’ AI smart glasses that listen and record every conversation
item.description: After developing a facial-recognition app for Meta’s Ray-Ban glasses and doxing random people, two former Harvard students are now launching a startup that makes smart glasses with an always-on microphone.

item.title: Meta to add 100MW of solar power from US gear
item.description: The social media company is adding another tranche of solar to power a new AI data center in South Carolina.

item.title: Perplexity accused of scraping websites that explicitly blocked AI scraping
item.description: Internet giant Cloudflare says it detected Perplexity crawling and scraping websites, even after customers had added technical blocks telling Perplexity not to scrape their pages.

item.title: Obvio’s stop sign cameras use AI to root out unsafe drivers
item.description: American streets are incredibly dangerous for pedestrians. A San Carlos, California-based startup called Obvio thinks it can change that by installing cameras at stop signs -- a solution the founders also say won’t create a panopticon.

item.title: Breakneck data center growth challenges Microsoft’s sustainability goals
item.description: Microsoft's sustainability goals are imperiled by its push into AI and cloud services.

item.title: Gridcare thinks more than 100 GW of data center capacity is hiding in the grid
item.description: Gridcare raised $13.3 million for its data platform that finds underutilized capacity on the electrical grid.

item.title: Meta adds another 650 MW of solar power to its AI push
item.description: The company already has more than 12 gigawatts of capacity in its renewable power portfolio.
